name: Kettlebell Suitcase Carry
resistance_profile: Anti-lateral-flexion: a load in one hand tries to bend you toward it, and the opposite-side obliques and QL isometrically brace to keep you upright as you walk.
cues: Don't lean toward or away from the weight
Brace as if bracing for a punch
execution_steps: Set a kettlebell at your side and deadlift it up to a straight-arm hang.
Stand tall: ribs down, shoulders level, abs braced.
Walk in a straight line with controlled steps, not letting the weight pull you sideways.
Cover the set distance or time, then set the bell down and repeat on the other side.
good_when: You want to train the obliques against real load with minimal setup
You want carryover to bracing and posture
bad_when: You have no space to walk
Grip fails long before the abs are challenged
common_errors: Leaning or hiking toward or away from the bell
Letting the loaded shoulder shrug or round forward
progressions: Increase distance or time under load
regressions: Shorter distance or a stationary hold
